text:
I we are given units of lineal measurements: Paramd^u (Atom),
Rajas (Dust-particle), Vdldgra (Tip of hair), a Nit, Louse, Barley, and
digit, each following measure being eight times the previous one,
Js'agnajit seems to have written on Drdvija architecture and sculpture.
There was also a system of measurement known as Drdvijamdna. But
this science was prevalent in the south long before Nagnajit, It is
not known whether he was a Digambara Jaina or one who had con-
quered them. Sage Vasiftha wrote on sculpture6. Varaha describes in
detail all the limbs of an idol from head to foot with their measure-
ments. The description of Srimahdvisnu is very entrancing and reads
like DhydnaUokas'7. In the list of Gods and Goddesses described here
there is no mention of Sarasvati, Kali or the Planets. There are some
common features between Chapters XLIII and LIX in respect of
trees,
